mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides a seamless local development environment for building and testing containerized applications using Kubernetes. It's ideal for developers who want to run Kubernetes clusters on their local machines without the complexity of managing a full cloud infrastructure.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
Download and install Docker Desktop for Mac from the official Docker website. Follow the installation prompts. After installation, launch Docker Desktop and enable Kubernetes in the settings.

1. Download Docker Desktop: [Docker Desktop for Mac](https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac)
2. Open Docker Desktop, go to Preferences > Kubernetes, and check 'Enable Kubernetes'.
3. Click 'Apply & Restart' to start the Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
After Docker Desktop restarts, verify that Kubernetes is running by executing:
```bash
kubectl cluster-info
```
You should see information about the Kubernetes master and services.

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
Ensure that kubectl is configured to use the Docker Desktop Kubernetes context:
```bash
kubectl config use-context docker-desktop
```
Verify the context:
```bash
kubectl config current-context
```
It should return 'docker-desktop'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
To enable resource autoscaling, apply the following configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/examples/master/staging/cluster-autoscaler/cluster-autoscaler.yaml
```
Make sure to adjust the parameters according to your needs.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
To completely remove Docker Desktop and its Kubernetes components:
1. Open Docker Desktop.
2. Go to Preferences > Uninstall.
3. Follow the prompts to uninstall.
4. Optionally, remove Docker Desktop application from the Applications folder and delete any associated files in `~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker`.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Docker Desktop to a newer version, download the latest version from the Docker website and install it over the existing version. Ensure that you back up any important data or configurations before upgrading. After upgrading, verify the Kubernetes version:
```bash
kubectl version --short
```
If you encounter issues, you can roll back to the previous version by reinstalling it from the Docker website.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes not starting
troubleshooting.description: If Kubernetes fails to start, check the Docker Desktop logs for errors. You can access logs by going to the Docker Desktop menu > Troubleshoot > Logs. Ensure that your system meets the minimum requirements for Docker Desktop.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl command not found
troubleshooting.description: If you receive a 'command not found' error when running kubectl, ensure that Docker Desktop is installed correctly and that the Docker Desktop binary path is added to your shell's PATH environment variable.

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits exceeded
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter resource limits errors, adjust the resource allocation for Docker Desktop in Preferences > Resources. Increase CPU and memory limits as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes context issues
troubleshooting.description:
If kubectl commands fail due to context issues, reset the context using:
```bash
kubectl config use-context docker-desktop
```

troubleshooting.title: Networking issues
troubleshooting.description: If you experience networking issues, ensure that your firewall settings allow Docker Desktop to communicate. You may also need to reset the network settings in Docker Desktop Preferences.

versionNotes.changes: Improved Kubernetes integration and performance enhancements.
Added support for Kubernetes 1.30 and various bug fixes.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of Docker Desktop Kubernetes will allow you to run a local Kubernetes cluster, manage it with kubectl, and deploy applications seamlessly. You should be able to verify the cluster status and configure additional resources as needed.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have macOS and sufficient resources to run Docker Desktop.
